purpose: PostToolUse hook on Write. Quality validator for progress file logs — enforces narrative depth on workers. 8 checks: (1) logs[] non-empty (hard warn), (2) message field >= 15 chars, (3) latest log entry >= 20 chars, (4) no lazy/vague patterns ('starting...', 'done', 'in progress', 'continuing', etc. — explicit blacklist), (5) minimum log count per stage (planning>=2, implementing>=3, testing>=4, finalizing/completed>=5), (6) no consecutive duplicate log messages, (7) milestones[] non-empty for stages implementing+, (8) files_changed[] non-empty for stages implementing+ (and especially completed). Aggregates all warnings into one message. Cannot block — purely coaching feedback.
gotchas: Lazy-pattern blacklist is case-insensitive but exact-string match — 'Starting work' (with extra word) passes; 'Starting...' alone fails
Stage thresholds are minimums — workers writing more is fine
PostToolUse: warn-only, cannot block; relies on agent to read the message and self-correct
completed stage with files_changed=0 is flagged as a hard quality issue ('the dashboard renders this as a clickable file list')
Single warn() call aggregates all WARNINGS — agent gets one big message per write
patterns: Multi-check aggregator with single emission at the end
Stage-progressive thresholds (more rigor required as the worker advances)
conventions: All checks emit '• ...' bullet items into a WARNINGS string
Final message prefixed with emoji '⚠️ PROGRESS LOG QUALITY:'
